title: Input & Output Coding
description: Hands-on coding challenges using Python's input/output functions, f-strings, and string formatting.
subject: Programming

content.prompt: Ask the user for their name and age. Then print a greeting message and tell them what year they will turn 100 years old. Use the current year as 2025.

# TODO: Calculate and print the year they turn 100
# Example: You will turn 100 in 2109!

testCases.expected:
Hello, Alice!
You will turn 100 in 2109!
testCases.description: Basic greeting with name Alice, age 16

Hello, Bob!
You will turn 100 in 2115!

content.prompt:
Ask the user for their first name, last name, and favorite color. Print a card using this EXACT format:

====================
Name: [first] [last]
Favorite Color: [color]
====================

Use string repetition for the border line (= repeated 20 times).
content.starterCode:
# TODO: Ask for first name, last name, and favorite color

# TODO: Print the card with borders
# Hint: use '=' * 20 for the border

testCases.expected:
====================
Name: Alex Smith
Favorite Color: Blue
====================
testCases.description: Card for Alex Smith who likes Blue

testCases.expected:
====================
Name: Maria Garcia
Favorite Color: Green
====================
testCases.description: Card for Maria Garcia who likes Green

content.prompt:
Ask the user for a distance in miles. Convert it to kilometers (1 mile = 1.609 km) and print the result rounded to 2 decimal places using an f-string.

Output format: [miles] miles = [km] km

# TODO: Convert to kilometers (1 mile = 1.609 km)
# TODO: Print using f-string with 2 decimal places
# Hint: f"{value:.2f}" formats to 2 decimal places

content.prompt:
Ask the user for a total number of seconds. Convert it to hours, minutes, and remaining seconds.

Output format:
[total] seconds = [h] hours, [m] minutes, [s] seconds

Hint: Use integer division (//) and modulus (%) operators.
